title: Chocolate Chip Delta Bars
ingredients: 2 egg yolks, slightly beaten with 1 tbsp water
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, beat butter with an electric mixer on high speed until creamy.
Beat in egg yolks and vanilla extract.
In a separate bowl, sift together flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
Add dry ingredients to creamed mixture and mix until stiff dough forms.
Press dough flat onto a greased 10 by 14 inch sheet and cover with chocolate chips.
Beat egg whites until very stiff.
Add brown sugar to egg whites and beat well to make meringue topping.
Spread meringue across top of dough, leaving the edges uncovered.
Return to oven for 12-15 minutes or until meringue is very light brown and dough is set.
Let cool and cut into bars.
notes: This is an adaptation of a family recipe originally made by Elly/Gma using shortening instead of butter and topped with a meringue with brown sugar.
The dough will be stiff after mixing the dry ingredients.
